display-lint — kill transcript clutter at the source

Playground for the new PreToolUse/Bash gate (brainstorm 2026-07-16, option A). CC renders every Bash call as a full tool card with no collapse setting (upstream #67005/#75500 open) — so the fix is to stop emitting wall-of-text commands in the first place.

Before / after

✕ BEFORE — one tool card, 229 chars, 7 stages, buries the narrative: cd /Users/yonatangross/coding/.../src/hooks/src && sed -n '1,40p' lib/env.ts && echo === && grep -n 'export function|STATE_FILE' lib/dev-stack-state.ts | head -10 && echo done ✓ AFTER — the hook denies it with a remedy; the model writes: bash /scratchpad/inspect-dev-stack.sh (steps live in the script; the transcript shows one short card)

Fire rule

ConditionThresholdWhy
Command length> 200 charsshort compounds are fine; only walls of text clutter
AND stage count≥ 3 segments (&& ; |)long single commands (e.g. git commit -m "…") are not clutter

Never fires on (the remedy must always pass)

ExemptionReason
heredoc commands (<<)writing a script file IS the fix
bash/sh/python/node <file> invocationsalready compliant
ORK_DISPLAY_LINT=0session escape hatch — pattern gates always have false positives

Verification

PASS 8 unit tests (incl. a real offender captured from a live transcript) PASS full suite 291 files / 7,187 tests · typecheck · biome PASS live probe through the BUILT dispatcher: long compound → deny · short control → silent · script control → silent

Dispatched via sync-bash-dispatcher (phase 2.5) · registry 199 → 200 · hooks.json 218 → 219 total.
